question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of concurrent GET /blocks requests with no query parameters, causing the default-limit DB query to saturate the PostgreSQL connection pool and degrade processing on >= 30% of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractLimitFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply limit values just below maxLimit repeatedly across many concurrent connections, bypassing the defaultLimit fallback and forcing the DB to execute maximum-row scans, exhausting DB CPU and degrading >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a 96-character hex hash prefix to trigger a LIKE-based full-table scan on the record_file table, causing sustained high-CPU DB queries that degrade >= 30% of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: validateHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a 64-character hex hash (0x-prefixed) that passes isValidEthHashOrHederaHash() and triggers a LIKE '%' suffix DB scan, causing repeated expensive sequential scans that degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both block.number and timestamp filters simultaneously, causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to build a multi-condition WHERE clause that forces a full index scan, and when repeated at scale degrades >= 30% of network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractOrderByFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user omit all where-filters so extractOrderByFromFilters() falls back to RecordFile.CONSENSUS_END ordering, causing the DB to perform a full-table ordered scan on every request, and when parallelized across many clients degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: generateNextLink()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination next link returned by generateNextLink() to chain unlimited sequential page requests, each consuming a DB query slot, causing sustained connection pool exhaustion that degrades >= 30% of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated requests with timestamp=gte:1 (minimum timestamp) and order=asc, forcing the DB to scan from the earliest record to the limit boundary on every request, causing sustained I/O that degrades >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple repeated timestamp filter parameters (e.g., timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:Y&timestamp=gte:Z) to cause extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to build an oversized whereQuery array, amplifying DB query complexity and degrading >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a block number of 0 (zero), which passes isPositiveLong(hashOrNumber, true) with allowZero=true, triggering a DB lookup for index=0 that may cause unexpected query behavior and when repeated at scale degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block.number=gte:0 combined with limit at maxLimit, forcing the DB to scan the entire record_file table from block 0 on every request, causing sustained full-table reads that degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractLimitFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply limit values exceeding maxLimit, causing extractLimitFromFilters() to silently fall back to defaultLimit rather than rejecting the request, allowing the attacker to probe the exact defaultLimit value and craft requests that maximize DB row fetches per query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a hash value consisting of all zeros (0x0000...0000, 64 hex chars) to trigger a LIKE '0000...0000%' scan that matches many rows due to hash prefix collisions, causing expensive multi-row DB scans that degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with order=asc and no other filters, causing the DB to perform a full forward scan of the record_file table ordered by consensus_end, and when issued concurrently from many clients degrade >= 30% of mirror node processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both block.number=gte:0 and timestamp=gte:1 filters simultaneously, causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to generate a two-condition WHERE clause that forces the DB to evaluate both index conditions per row, amplifying CPU usage and degrading >= 30% of nodes at scale?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on the /blocks endpoint to send thousands of concurrent requests per second, each triggering a DB query via RecordFileService.getBlocks(), exhausting the DB connection pool and degrading >= 30% of network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: generateNextLink()] Can an unprivileged external user follow the next pagination link in a tight loop without any delay, causing a sustained sequence of DB queries that keeps the connection pool saturated and degrades >= 30% of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: validateHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a 96-character hex string (Hedera hash format) that passes isValidEthHashOrHederaHash() and triggers a LIKE scan with a 48-byte prefix, causing the DB to perform expensive prefix-matching scans on the hash column and degrade >= 30% of nodes when repeated at scale?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with timestamp=lte:9999999999 (far-future timestamp) and order=desc, causing the DB to scan from the latest block backward through the entire table up to the limit, and when parallelized degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractOrderFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send multiple order parameters (e.g., order=asc&order=desc) causing findLast() to pick the last value, and by alternating order values across many requests force the DB query planner to switch between ascending and descending index scans, degrading query plan caching and impacting >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a block number equal to maxLong (9223372036854775807) to trigger a DB lookup for a non-existent index, and when repeated at scale cause repeated unnecessary DB round-trips that degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block.number=lte:9223372036854775807 (maxLong) and limit at maxLimit, forcing the DB to scan the entire record_file table up to the limit on every request, causing sustained high-I/O that degrades >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum allowed number of repeated timestamp query parameters to cause extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to generate a WHERE clause with many AND conditions, causing the DB to evaluate all conditions per row and amplifying CPU usage enough to degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block.number=gte:0&block.number=lte:9223372036854775807 to force a full-table scan bounded only by the limit, and when issued concurrently from distributed clients degrade >= 30% of mirror node processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a hash with only the 0x prefix and a single hex character (e.g., 0x0 padded to 64 chars) to trigger a LIKE scan that matches a large number of hash prefixes, causing expensive multi-row DB scans that degrade >= 30% of nodes?
